import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut current = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      else if b == 45
        neg = true
      else
        if has
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, -current)
          else
            result = list.push(result, current)
          end
          current = 0
          has = false
          neg = false
        end
      end
    when None
      i = n
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, -current)
    else
      result = list.push(result, current)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len() - 1
  while lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        return Some(mid)
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      lo = hi + 1
    end
  end
  None
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let xs = parse_ints(line1)
let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(n)
  n
when None
  0
end

match bsearch(xs, target)
when Some(i)
  println("index=#{i}")
when None
  println("not_found")
end
